methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series D → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.5x) to $1.0B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 8 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Space & Aerospace. Range: $1.0B - $3.5B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 64.2x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Stoke Space is a Series D-stage space and aerospace company founded by Andy Lapsa and Tom Feldman, currently valued at $1.0B post-money following $860M in total capital raised. The valuation assessment relies on two applicable triangulation methods drawn from a peer set of 8 private companies at the same or adjacent stage—including Astranis ($3.5B Series D), ICEYE ($2.8B Series E), Axiom Space ($2.5B Series C), Impulse Space ($1.8B Series C), and Varda Space Industries ($1.5B Series C)—alongside 5 public Space & Aerospace comparables (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL).

Stage-progression analysis applies a typical 1.5x step-up multiplier to the $1.0B Series D post-money valuation, implying $1.5B. Peer-median valuation across the 8-company cohort yields $1.6B, with a range spanning $1.0B to $3.5B. Public-company anchoring via P/S multiples cannot be applied due to absence of revenue disclosure, a standard limitation for private-company analysis. These two applicable methods converge tightly: stage-progression at $1.5B and peer-median at $1.6B.

The triangulated valuation range is $1.3B (low) to $1.9B (high), with a median of $1.6B. This range sits modestly above the current $1.0B valuation and reflects typical Series D pricing within the space sector. Key uncertainties include the revenue profile and capital efficiency relative to peers, neither available in current data, which would refine any revenue-multiple assessment.
